sectiondef.header: Configuration options

description.para: The following are device configuration options.
See the XAxiEthernet_SetOptions, XAxiEthernet_ClearOptions and XAxiEthernet_GetOptions routines for information on how to use options.
The default state of the options are also noted below.

briefdescription.para: < XAE_PROMISC_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to accept all incoming packets.

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to disabled (cleared) by default.XAE_JUMBO_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to accept jumbo frames for transmit and receive. This driver sets this option to disabled (cleared) by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_VLAN_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to enable VLAN support for transmit and receive.

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to disabled (cleared) by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_FLOW_CONTROL_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to recognize received flow control frames.

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to enabled (set) by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_FCS_STRIP_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to strip FCS and PAD from received frames.

detaileddescription.para: Note that PAD from VLAN frames is not stripped. This driver sets this option to enabled (set) by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_FCS_INSERT_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to generate the FCS field and add PAD automatically for outgoing frames.

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to enabled (set) by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_LENTYPE_ERR_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to enable Length/Type error checking (mismatched type/length field) for received frames.

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to enabled (set) by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_TRANSMITTER_ENABLE_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device transmitter to be enabled.

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to enabled (set) by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_RECEIVER_ENABLE_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device receiver to be enabled.

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to enabled (set) by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_BROADCAST_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to receive frames sent to the broadcast Ethernet address.

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to enabled (set) by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_MULTICAST_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to receive frames sent to Ethernet addresses that are programmed into the Multicast Address Table (MAT).

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to disabled (cleared) by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_EXT_MULTICAST_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to receive frames sent to Ethernet addresses that are programmed into the Multicast Address Table.

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to be dependent on HW configuration by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_EXT_TXVLAN_TRAN_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to enable transmit VLAN translation.

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to be dependent on HW configuration by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_EXT_RXVLAN_TRAN_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to enable receive VLAN translation.

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to be dependent on HW configuration by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_EXT_TXVLAN_TAG_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to enable transmit VLAN tagging.

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to be dependent during HW build time by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_EXT_RXVLAN_TAG_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to enable receive VLAN tagging.

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to be dependent during HW build time by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_EXT_TXVLAN_STRP_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to enable transmit VLAN stripping.

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to be dependent during HW build time by default.

briefdescription.para: XAE_EXT_RXVLAN_STRP_OPTION specifies the Axi Ethernet device to enable receive VLAN stripping.

detaileddescription.para: This driver sets this option to be dependent during HW build time by default.

memberdef.name: XAE_DEFAULT_OPTIONS
memberdef.initializer: (XAE_FLOW_CONTROL_OPTION | \ XAE_BROADCAST_OPTION | \ XAE_FCS_INSERT_OPTION | \ XAE_FCS_STRIP_OPTION | \ XAE_LENTYPE_ERR_OPTION | \ XAE_TRANSMITTER_ENABLE_OPTION | \ XAE_RECEIVER_ENABLE_OPTION)

briefdescription.para: XAE_DEFAULT_OPTIONS specify the options set in XAxiEthernet_Reset() and XAxiEthernet_CfgInitialize()

This typedef contains configuration information for a Axi Ethernet device.
struct XAxiEthernet is the type for Axi Ethernet driver instance data.

detaileddescription.para: The calling code is required to use a unique instance of this structure for every Axi Ethernet device used in the system. A reference to a structure of this type is then passed to the driver API functions.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_CfgInitialize initializes an AXI Ethernet device along with the InstancePtr that references it.

detaileddescription.para: The PHY is setup independently from the Ethernet core. Use the MII or whatever other interface may be present for setup.

parameterdescription.para: references the memory instance to be associated with the AXI Ethernet core instance upon initialization.
references the structure holding the hardware configuration for the Axi Ethernet core to initialize.

is the processor address used to access the base address of the AXI Ethernet instance. In systems with an MMU and virtual memory, EffectiveAddress is the virtual address mapped to the physical in ConfigPtr->Config.BaseAddress. In systems without an active MMU, EffectiveAddress should be set to the same value as ConfigPtr->Config.BaseAddress.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_Initialize initializes an AXI Ethernet device along with the InstancePtr that references it.

detaileddescription.para: The PHY is setup independently from the Ethernet core. Use the MII or whatever other interface may be present for setup.

parameterdescription.para: references the memory instance to be associated with the AXI Ethernet core instance upon initialization.
references the structure holding the hardware configuration for the Axi Ethernet core to initialize.

is the processor address used to access the base address of the AXI Ethernet instance. In systems with an MMU and virtual memory, EffectiveAddress is the virtual address mapped to the physical in ConfigPtr->Config.BaseAddress. In systems without an active MMU, EffectiveAddress should be set to the same value as ConfigPtr->Config.BaseAddress.

simplesect.para: When user calls this function he should ensure the hardware is in a quiescent state by resetting all the hardware Configurations.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_Start starts the Axi Ethernet device as follows:

para.verbatim: - Enable transmitter if XAE_TRANSMIT_ENABLE_OPTION is set - Enable receiver if XAE_RECEIVER_ENABLE_OPTION is set

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_Stop gracefully stops the Axi Ethernet device as follows:

para.verbatim: - Disable all interrupts from this device - Disable the receiver

detaileddescription.para: XAxiEthernet_Stop does not modify any of the current device options.
Since the transmitter is not disabled, frames currently in internal buffers or in process by a DMA engine are allowed to be transmitted.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_Reset does not perform a soft reset of the AxiEthernet core.

detaileddescription.para: AxiEthernet hardware is reset by the device connected to the AXI4-Stream interface. This function inserts some delay before proceeding to check for MgtRdy bit. The delay is necessary to be at a safe side. It takes a while for the reset process to complete and for any of the AxiEthernet registers to be accessed. It then checks for MgtRdy bit in IS register to know if AxiEthernet reset is completed or not. Subsequently it calls one more driver function to complete the AxiEthernet hardware initialization.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: It is the responsibility of the user to reset the AxiEthernet hardware before using it. AxiEthernet hardware should be reset through the device connected to the AXI4-Stream interface of AxiEthernet.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_SetMacAddress sets the MAC address for the Axi Ethernet device, specified by InstancePtr to the MAC address specified by AddressPtr.

detaileddescription.para: The Axi Ethernet device must be stopped before calling this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
is a reference to the 6-byte MAC address to set.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on successful completion.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the Axi Ethernet device has not stopped,

simplesect.para: This routine also supports the extended/new VLAN and multicast mode. The XAE_RAF_NEWFNCENBL_MASK bit dictates which offset will be configured.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_GetMacAddress gets the MAC address for the Axi Ethernet, specified by InstancePtr into the memory buffer specified by AddressPtr.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
AddressPtr
references the memory buffer to store the retrieved MAC address. This memory buffer must be at least 6 bytes in length.
None.
This routine also supports the extended/new VLAN and multicast mode. The XAE_RAF_NEWFNCENBL_MASK bit dictates which offset will be configured.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_UpdateDepOption check and update dependent options for new/extended features.

detaileddescription.para: This is a helper function that is meant to be called by XAxiEthernet_SetOptions() and XAxiEthernet_ClearOptions().

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
Dependent options that are required to set/clear per hardware requirement.
This helper function collects the dependent OPTION(s) per hardware design. When conflicts arises, extended features have precedence over legacy ones. Two operations to be considered,
Adding extended options. If XATE_VLAN_OPTION is enabled and enable one of extended VLAN options, XATE_VLAN_OPTION should be off and configure to hardware. However, axi-ethernet instance Options variable still holds XATE_VLAN_OPTION so when all of the extended feature are removed, XATE_VLAN_OPTION can be effective and configured to hardware.
Removing extended options. Remove extended option can not just remove the selected extended option and dependent options. All extended options need to be verified and remained when one or more extended options are enabled.

Dependent options are :
XAE_VLAN_OPTION,
XAE_JUMBO_OPTION
XAE_FCS_INSERT_OPTION,
XAE_FCS_STRIP_OPTION
XAE_PROMISC_OPTION.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_SetOptions enables the options, Options for the Axi Ethernet, specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: Axi Ethernet should be stopped with XAxiEthernet_Stop() before changing options.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
is a bitmask of OR'd XAE_*_OPTION values for options to set. Options not specified are not affected.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on successful completion.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device has not been stopped.

simplesect.para: See xaxiethernet.h for a description of the available options.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_ClearOptions clears the options, Options for the Axi Ethernet, specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: Axi Ethernet should be stopped with XAxiEthernet_Stop() before changing options.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
is a bitmask of OR'd XAE_*_OPTION values for options to clear. Options not specified are not affected.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on successful completion.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device has not been stopped.

simplesect.para: See xaxiethernet.h for a description of the available options.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_GetOptions returns the current option settings.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Returns a bitmask of XAE_*_OPTION constants, each bit specifying an option that is currently active.
See xaxiethernet.h for a description of the available options.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_GetOperatingSpeed gets the current operating link speed.

detaileddescription.para: This may be the value set by XAxiEthernet_SetOperatingSpeed() or a hardware default.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Returns the link speed in units of megabits per second (10 / 100 / 1000). Can return a value of 0, in case it does not get a valid speed from EMMC.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_SetOperatingSpeed sets the current operating link speed.

detaileddescription.para: For any traffic to be passed, this speed must match the current MII/GMII/SGMII/RGMII link speed.

parameterdescription.para: references the Axi Ethernet on which to operate.
is the speed to set in units of Mbps. Valid values are 10, 100, or 1000.

simplesect.para:
- XST_SUCCESS on successful setting of speed.
XST_FAILURE, if the speed cannot be set for the present hardware configuration.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_SetBadFrmRcvOption is used to enable the bad frame receive option.

detaileddescription.para: If enabled, this option ensures that bad receive frames are allowed and passed to the AXI4-Stream interface as if they are good frames.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_ClearBadFrmRcvOption is used to disable the bad frame receive option.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_DisableControlFrameLenCheck is used to disable the length check for control frames (pause frames).

detaileddescription.para: This means once the API is called, control frames larger than the minimum frame length are accepted.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_EnableControlFrameLenCheck is used to enable the length check for control frames (pause frames).

detaileddescription.para: After calling the API, all control frames received will be checked for proper length (less than minimum frame length). By default, upon normal start up, control frame length check is enabled. Hence this API needs to be called only if previously the control frame length check has been disabled by calling the API XAxiEthernet_DisableControlFrameLenCheck.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_PhySetMdioDivisor sets the MDIO clock divisor in the Axi Ethernet,specified by InstancePtr to the value, Divisor.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called once after each reset prior to accessing MII PHY registers.
From the Virtex-6(TM) and Spartan-6 (TM) Embedded Tri-Mode Ethernet MAC User's Guide, the following equation governs the MDIO clock to the PHY:

where f[HOSTCLK] is the bus clock frequency in MHz, and f[MDC] is the MDIO clock frequency in MHz to the PHY. Typically, f[MDC] should not exceed 2.5 MHz. Some PHYs can tolerate faster speeds which means faster access.

parameterdescription.para: references the Axi Ethernet instance on which to operate.
is the divisor value to set within the range of 0 to XAE_MDIO_MC_CLK_DVD_MAX.

memberdef.argsstring: (XAxiEthernet *InstancePtr, u32 PhyAddress, u32 RegisterNum, u16 PhyData)
memberdef.name: XAxiEthernet_PhyWrite

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_Get_Phy_Interface returns the type of PHY interface being used by the given instance, specified by InstancePtr.

detaileddescription.para: XAxiEthernet_GetPhysicalInterface returns the type of PHY interface being used by the given instance, specified by InstancePtr.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: The Physical Interface type which is one of XAE_PHY_TYPE_x where x is MII, GMII, RGMII_1_3, RGMII_2_0, SGMII, or 1000BASE_X (defined in xaxiethernet.h).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: The Physical Interface type which is one of XAE_PHY_TYPE_x where x is MII, GMII, RGMII_1_3, RGMII_2_0, SGMII, or 1000BASE_X (defined in xaxiethernet.h).

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: A table contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration table entry corresponding to the given device ID/BaseAddress, or NULL if no match is found.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_SetMacPauseAddress sets the MAC address used for pause frames to AddressPtr.

detaileddescription.para: AddressPtr will be the address the Axi Ethernet device will recognize as being for pause frames. Pause frames transmitted with XAxiEthernet_SendPausePacket() will also use this address.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
is a pointer to the 6-byte Ethernet address to set.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on successful completion.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the Axi Ethernet device is not stopped.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_GetMacPauseAddress gets the MAC address used for pause frames for the Axi Ethernet device specified by InstancePtr.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
references the memory buffer to store the retrieved MAC address. This memory buffer must be at least 6 bytes in length.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_SendPausePacket sends a pause packet with the value of PauseValue.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
is the pause value in units of 512 bit times.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on successful completion.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the Axi Ethernet device is not stopped.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_GetSgmiiStatus get the state of the link when using the SGMII media interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
references the location to store the result, which is the auto negotiated link speed in units of Mbits/sec, either 0, 10, 100, or 1000.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on successful completion.
XST_NO_FEATURE if the Axi Ethernet device is not using an SGMII interface,

simplesect.para: Currently SGMII PHY does not support half duplex mode.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_GetRgmiiStatus get the state of the link when using the RGMII media interface.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
references the location to store the result, which is the auto negotiated link speed in units of Mbits/sec, either 0, 10, 100, or 1000.
references the value that is set by this function to indicate full duplex operation. IsFullDuplexPtr is set to TRUE when the RGMII link is operating in full duplex mode, otherwise it is set to FALSE.
references the value that is set by this function to indicate the link status.IsLinkUpPtr is set to TRUE when the RGMII link up, otherwise it is set to FALSE.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on successful completion.
XST_NO_FEATURE if the Axi Ethernet device is not using an RGMII interface,

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_MulticastAdd adds the Ethernet address, AddressPtr to the Axi Ethernet device's multicast filter list, at list index Entry.

detaileddescription.para: The address referenced by AddressPtr may be of any unicast, multicast, or broadcast address form. The hardware for the Axi Ethernet device can hold up to C_Number_of_Table_Entries addresses in this filter list.
The device must be stopped to use this function.

Once an Ethernet address is programmed, the Axi Ethernet device will begin receiving data sent from that address. The Axi Ethernet hardware does not have a control bit to disable multicast filtering. The only way to prevent the Axi Ethernet device from receiving messages from an Ethernet address in the Multicast Address Table (MAT) is to clear it with XAxiEthernet_MulticastClear().

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
AddressPtr
is a pointer to the 6-byte Ethernet address to set. The previous address at the location Entry (if any) is overwritten with the value at AddressPtr.
Entry
is the hardware storage location to program this address and must be between 0 to (C_Number_of_Table_Entries - 1).
XST_SUCCESS on successful completion.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED.if the Axi Ethernet device is not stopped.
This routine works only with normal multicast filtering feature. A maximum of 4 multicast addresses can be stored in the HW provided multicast table.

To use the extended multicast feature, extended multicast filtering must be enabled by using driver API XAxiEthernet_SetOptions with proper option fields set. Once extended multicast filtering is enabled, the APIs XAxiEthernet_[Add|Clear|Get]ExtMulticastGroup() must be used to manage multicast address groups.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_MulticastGet gets the Ethernet address stored at index Entry in the Axi Ethernet device's multicast filter list.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
AddressPtr
references the memory buffer to store the retrieved Ethernet address. This memory buffer must be at least 6 bytes in length.
Entry
is the hardware storage location from which to retrieve the address and must be between 0 to (C_Number_of_Table_Entries - 1)
None.
This routine works only with normal multicast filtering feature. A maximum of 4 multicast addresses can be stored in the HW provided multicast table.

To use the extended multicast feature, extended multicast filtering must be enabled by using driver API XAxiEthernet_SetOptions with proper option fields set. Once extended multicast filtering is enabled, the APIs XAxiEthernet_[Add|Clear|Get]ExtMulticastGroup() must be used to manage multicast address groups.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_MulticastClear clears the Ethernet address stored at index Entry in the Axi Ethernet device's multicast filter list.

detaileddescription.para: The device must be stopped to use this function.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
Entry
is the HW storage location used when this address was added. It must be between 0 to (C_Number_of_Table_Entries - 1).
XST_SUCCESS on successful completion.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED.if the Axi Ethernet device is not stopped.
This routine works only with normal multicast filtering feature. A maximum of 4 multicast addresses can be stored in the HW provided multicast table.

To use the extended multicast feature, extended multicast filtering must be enabled by using driver API XAxiEthernet_SetOptions with proper option fields set. Once extended multicast filtering is enabled, the APIs XAxiEthernet_[Add|Clear|Get]ExtMulticastGroup() must be used to manage multicast address groups.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_SetTpid sets the VLAN Tag Protocol Identifier(TPID).

detaileddescription.para: Four values can be configured - 0x8100, 0x9100, 0x9200, 0x88A8.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
is a hex value to be added to the TPID table. The four values that can be added are 0x8100, 0x9100, 0x9200, 0x88A8.
is the hardware storage location to program this address and must be between 0..XAE_TPID_MAX_ENTRIES.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on successful completion.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED, if the Axi Ethernet device is not stopped.
XST_NO_FEATURE if the Axi Ethernet does not enable or have the VLAN tag capability.
XST_INVALID_PARAM if Tpid is not one of supported values.

simplesect.para: The device must be stopped to use this function.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_ClearTpid clears the VLAN Tag Protocol Identifier(TPID).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
is the hardware storage location to program this address and must be between 0..XAE_TPID_MAX_ENTRIES.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS on successful completion.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the Axi Ethernet device is not stopped.
XST_NO_FEATURE if the Axi Ethernet does not enable or have the VLAN tag capability.

simplesect.para: The device must be stopped to use this function.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_GetTpid gets the VLAN Tag Protocol Identifier value (TPID).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
references the location to store the result.
is the hardware storage location to program this address and must be between 0..XAE_TPID_MAX_ENTRIES.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_SetVTagMode configures the VLAN tagging mode.

detaileddescription.para:
Four modes can be configured,
XAE_VTAG_NONE for no tagging.
XAE_VTAG_ALL to tag all frames.
XAE_VTAG_EXISTED to tag already tagged frames.
XAE_VTAG_SELECT to tag selected already tagged frames based on VID value.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
Mode
is the VLAN tag mode. Value must be between b'00-b'11.
Dir
must be either XAE_TX or XAE_RX.
XST_SUCCESS. on successful completion.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the Axi Ethernet device is not stopped.
XST_NO_FEATURE if the Axi Ethernet does not enable or have the TX VLAN tag capability.
XST_INVALID_PARAM if Mode is not one of supported modes.
The device must be stopped to use this function.

The fourth mode (specified by XAE_VTAG_SELECT) requires a method for specifying which tagged frames should receive an additional VLAN tag. The VLAN translation table 'tag enabled' is referenced. That configuration is handled in XAxiEthernet_SetVidTable().
Mode value shifting is handled in this function. No shifting is required to call this function.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_GetVTagMode gets VLAN tagging mode.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
ModePtr
references the location to store the VLAN tag mode. Value is between b'00-b'11.
Dir
must be either XAE_TX or XAE_RX.
None.
The device must be stopped to use this function.
Mode value shifting is handled in this function. No shifting is required to call this function.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_SetVStripMode configures the VLAN strip mode.

detaileddescription.para:
Three modes can be configured :
XAE_VSTRP_NONE for no stripping.
XAE_VSTRP_ALL to strip one tag from all frames.
XAE_VSTRP_SELECT to strip one tag from already selected tagged frames based on VID value.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
Mode
is the VLAN strip mode. Value must be b'00, b'01, or b'11.
Dir
must be either XAE_TX or XAE_RX.
XST_SUCCESS on successful completion., returns XST_SUCCESS.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the Axi Ethernet device is not stopped.
XST_NO_FEATURE if the Axi Ethernet does not enable or have the TX VLAN strip capability.
XST_INVALID_PARAM if Mode is not one of supported modes.
The device must be stopped to use this function.
The third mode (specified by XAE_VSTRP_SELECT) requires a method for specifying which tagged frames should be stripped. The VLAN translation table 'stripped enabled' is referenced. That configuration is handled in XAxiEthernet_SetVidTable().
Mode value shifting is handled in this function. No shifting is required to call this function.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_GetVStripMode gets the VLAN stripping mode.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
ModePtr
references the location to store the VLAN strip mode returned by this function. Value is b'00, b'01 or b'11. Refer XAE_VTSRAP_* in xaxiethernet.h file for the details.
Dir
must be either XAE_TX or XAE_RX.
None.
Mode value shifting is handled in this function. No shifting is required to call this function.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_SetVTagValue configures the VLAN tagging value.

detaileddescription.para: The device must be stopped to use this function.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
VTagValue
is the VLAN tag value to be configured. A 32bit value. TPID, one of the following 16 bit values, 0x8100, 0x88a8, 0x9100, 0x9200. Priority, 3 bits CFI, 1 bit VID, 12 bits
Dir
must be either XAE_TX or XAE_RX.
XST_SUCCESS on successful completion, returns .
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the Axi Ethernet device is not stopped.
XST_NO_FEATURE if the Axi Ethernet does not enable/have TX VLAN tag capability.
XST_INVALID_PARAM, if the TPID is not one the four supported values.
The four supported TPID values are 0x8100, 0x88a8, 0x9100, 0x9200. XAxiEthernet_SetVTagValue performs verification on TPID only.
Ethernet VLAN frames' VLAN type/length(2B) and tag control information(2B). Bit layout : bbbb bbbb bbbb bbbb bbb b bbbb bbbb bbbb \ / | | \ VID (12b) / \ / | CFI bit (1b) TPID (16b) priority bit (3b)

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_GetVTagValue gets the configured VLAN tagging value.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
VTagValuePtr
references the location to store the result. Format is TPID, one of the following 16 bit values, 0x8100, 0x88a8, 0x9100, 0x9200. Priority, 3 bits CFI, 1 bit VID, 12 bits
Dir
must be either XAE_TX or XAE_RX.
None.
Ethernet VLAN frames' VLAN type/length(2B) and tag control information(2B). Bit layout : bbbb bbbb bbbb bbbb bbb b bbbb bbbb bbbb \ / | | \ VID (12b) / \ / | CFI bit (1b) TPID (16b) priority bit (3b)

memberdef.argsstring: (XAxiEthernet *InstancePtr, u32 Entry, u32 Vid, u8 Strip, u8 Tag, int Dir)
memberdef.name: XAxiEthernet_SetVidTable

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_SetVidTable sets VID table includes new VLAN ID, strip and tag enable bits.

detaileddescription.para: The device must be stopped to use this function.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
Entry
is the hardware storage location/index to program updated VID value, strip, or tag value. The value must be between 0..0xFFF.
Vid
is updated/translated Vid value to be programmed.
Strip
is strip enable indication for Vid.
Tag
is tag enable indication for Vid.
Dir
must be either XAE_TX or XAE_RX.
XST_SUCCESS on successful completion.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the Axi Ethernet device is not stopped.
XST_NO_FEATURE if the Axi Ethernet does not enable/have extended functionalities.
The hardware requires the table to be 'indexed' with Entry and must be 0x000..0xFFF.
Bits layout is bbbb bbbb bbbb b b VLAN ID (12b), | | | VLAN double tag enable bit VLAN strip enable bit
To disable translation indexed by Entry, Set Vid = Entry.

memberdef.argsstring: (XAxiEthernet *InstancePtr, u32 Entry, u32 *VidPtr, u8 *StripPtr, u8 *TagPtr, int Dir)
memberdef.name: XAxiEthernet_GetVidTable

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_GetVidTable gets VID table content includes new VLAN ID, strip and tag enable bits.

detaileddescription.para: The device must be stopped to use this function.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
Entry
is the hardware storage location/index to program updated VID value, strip, or tag value. The value must be between 0..0xFFF.
VidPtr
references the location to store the result. This function stores the Vid value indexed by Entry into this location.
StripPtr
references the location to store the result. This function stores the strip enable bit value indexed by Entry into this location.
TagPtr
references the location to store the result. This function stores the tag enable bit value indexed by Entry into this location.
Dir
must be either XAE_TX or XAE_RX.
None.
The hardware requires the table to be 'indexed' with Entry and must be 0x000..0xFFF.
Bits layout is bbbb bbbb bbbb b b VLAN ID (12b), | | | VLAN double tag enable bit VLAN strip enable bit

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_AddExtMulticastGroup adds an entry to the multicast Ethernet address table.

detaileddescription.para: The new entry, represents a group of MAC addresses based on the contents of AddressPtr. AddressPtr is one member of the MAC address set in the newly added entry.
The device must be stopped to use this function.

Once an Ethernet address is programmed, the Axi Ethernet device will begin receiving data sent from that address. The Axi Ethernet hardware does not have a control bit to disable multicast filtering. The only way to prevent the Axi Ethernet device from receiving messages from an Ethernet address in the multicast table is to clear it with XAxiEthernet_ClearExtMulticastGroup().

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
AddressPtr
is a pointer to the 6-byte Ethernet address to add.
XST_SUCCESS.on successful completion.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the Axi Ethernet device is not stopped.
XST_INVALID_PARAM if the input MAC address is not between 01:00:5E:00:00:00 and 01:00:5E:7F:FF:FF per RFC1112.
This routine consider all 2**23 possible multicast Ethernet addresses to be 8Mx1 bit or 1M bytes memory area. All defined multicast addresses are from 01.00.5E.00.00.00 to 01.00.5E.7F.FF.FF The most significant 25 bit out of 48 bit are static, so they will not be part of calculation.
The hardware requires to 'index' with bit 22-8, 15 bits in total. The least significant byte/8 bits are considered a group.
This API operates at a group (256 MAC addresses) for hardware to do the first layer address filtering. It is user's responsibility to provision this table appropriately.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_ClearExtMulticastGroup clears input multicast Ethernet address group from table.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
AddressPtr
is a pointer to the 6-byte Ethernet address to clear.
XST_SUCCESS on successful completion, returns XST_SUCCESS.
XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the Axi Ethernet device is not stopped
XST_INVALID_PARAM if input MAC address is not between 01:00:5E:00:00:00 and 01:00:5E:7F:FF:FF per RFC1112.
Please reference XAxiEthernet_AddExtMulticastGroup for multicast address index and bit value calculation.
In table, hardware requires to 'index' with bit 22-8, 15 bits in total. The least significant byte/8 bits are considered a group.

There is a scenario that might introduce issues: When multicast tables are programmed initially to accept 01:00:5E:12:34:56 and 01:00:5E:12:34:78 but later decided to clear 01:00:5E:12:34:78. Without validating all possible combinations at the indexed entry, multicast table might be misconfigured and drop frames.
When clearing a multicast address table entry, note that a whole group of mac addresses will no longer be accepted - this because an entry in the table represents multiple(256) mac addresses.

The device must be stopped to use this function.
This API operates at a group (256 MAC addresses) level for hardware to perform the first layer address filtering. It is user's responsibility to provision this table appropriately.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_GetExtMulticastGroup returns whether the given Ethernet address group is stored in the table.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
AddressPtr
is a pointer to the 6-byte Ethernet address.
TRUE if it is an acceptable multicast MAC address and the group is present in the table.
FALSE if it is not a valid multicast MAC address or the group was not found in the table.
In the table, hardware requires to 'index' with bit 22-8, 15 bits in total. The least significant byte/8 bits are considered a group. This API operates at a group (256 MAC addresses) level.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_DumpExtMulticastGroup dumps ALL provisioned acceptable multicast MAC in the Axi Ethernet device's multicast table.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
None.
Hardware requires to 'index' with bit 22-8, 15 bits in total. The least significant byte/8 bits are considered a set.
This API operates at a set (256 MAC addresses) level.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_IsStarted reports if the device is in the started or stopped state.

detaileddescription.para: To be in the started state, the calling code must have made a successful call to XAxiEthernet_Start. To be in the stopped state, XAxiEthernet_Stop or XAxiEthernet_CfgInitialize function must have been called.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the of Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: TRUE if the device has been started.
FALSE.if the device has not been started

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_GetIntStatus returns a bit mask of the interrupt status register (ISR).

detaileddescription.para: XAxiEthernet_GetIntStatus can be used to query the status without having to have interrupts enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Returns a bit mask of the interrupt status conditions. The mask will be a set of bit wise or'd values from the XAE_INT_*_MASK definitions in xaxitemac_hw.h file.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_IntEnable enables the interrupts specified in Mask.

detaileddescription.para: The corresponding interrupt for each bit set to 1 in Mask, will be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
contains a bit mask of the interrupts to enable. The mask can be formed using a set of bit wise or'd values from the XAE_INT_*_MASK definitions in xaxitemac_hw.h file.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiEthernet_IntEnable(XAxiEthernet *InstancePtr, u32 Mask)

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_IntDisable disables the interrupts specified in Mask.

detaileddescription.para: The corresponding interrupt for each bit set to 1 in Mask, will be disabled. In other words, XAxiEthernet_IntDisable uses the "set a bit to clear it" scheme.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
contains a bit mask of the interrupts to disable. The mask can be formed using a set of bit wise or'd values from the XAE_INT_*_MASK definitions in xaxitemac_hw.h file

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiEthernet_IntDisable(XAxiEthernet *InstancePtr, u32 Mask)

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_IntPending returns a bit mask of the pending interrupts.

detaileddescription.para: Each bit set to 1 in the return value represents a pending interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.

simplesect.para: Returns a bit mask of the interrupts that are pending. The mask will be a set of bit wise or'd values from the XAE_INT_*_MASK definitions in xaxitemac_hw.h file.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_IntClear clears pending interrupts specified in Mask.

detaileddescription.para: The corresponding pending interrupt for each bit set to 1 in Mask, will be cleared. In other words, XAxiEthernet_IntClear uses the "set a bit to clear it" scheme.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.
contains a bit mask of the pending interrupts to clear. The mask can be formed using a set of bit wise or'd values from the XAE_INT_*_MASK definitions in xaxitemac_hw.h file.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiEthernet_IntClear(XAxiEthernet *InstancePtr, u32 Mask)

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_IsStatsConfigured returns determines if Statistics gathering.

detaileddescription.para: is configured in the hardware or not.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the Axi Ethernet instance to be worked on.

listitem.para: TRUE if the device is configured with statistics gathering.
FALSE if the device is NOT configured with statistics gathering.

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_ReadReg returns the value read from the register specified by RegOffset.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the Axi Ethernet device.
is the offset of the register to be read.

simplesect.para: Returns the 32-bit value of the register.
C-style signature: u32 XAxiEthernet_ReadReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

briefdescription.para: XAxiEthernet_WriteReg, writes Data to the register specified by RegOffset.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the Axi Ethernet device.
is the offset of the register to be written.
is the 32-bit value to write to the register.

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XAxiEthernet_WriteReg(u32 BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: The APIs in this file takes care of the primary functionalities of the driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains static initialization functionality for Axi Ethernet driver.
This is a guide on how to move from using the ll temac driver to use the xaxiethernet driver.
This header file contains identifiers and macros that can be used to access the Axi Ethernet device.
This file has driver APIs related to the controlling of the extended features of the AXI Ethernet device.

The APIs in this driver take care of the following:
Starting or stopping the Axi Ethernet device
Initializing and resetting the Axi Ethernet device
Setting MAC address and speed/duplex of the device
Provide means for controlling the PHY and communicating with it.
Turn on/off various features/options provided by the Axi Ethernet device. See xaxiethernet.h for a detailed description of the driver. MODIFICATION HISTORY:

Changed the prototype of XAxiEthernet_CfgInitialize API. 5.7 srm 01/16/18 Implemented poll timeout API which replaces while loops to ensure a deterministic time delay. 5.8 rsp 07/20/18 Fix cppcheck warning in Aptr assignment. 5.10 aru 08/16/19 Fix coverity warning

detaileddescription.para:
Please note that APIs for turning on/off any of the driver features are present in axiethernet.c. This file takes care of controlling these features.
Normal/extended multicast filtering
Normal/extended VLAN features
RGMII/SGMII features
See xaxiethernet.h for a detailed description of the driver.

detaileddescription.para:
The AXI Ethernet IP is based on the XPS_LL TEMAC IP. There are few changes in the IP and some of them are listed below
The Interface to access the IP is now AXI instead of the PLBV46
AXI4 streaming interfaces is used instead of LL (Local Link)
All indirect accesses to Ethernet core registers have been removed.
The AxiEthernet reset line is connected to the reset line of the device connected to the AXI4-Stream interface. Hence any reset of the connected device would reset AxiEthernet.
Please read the HW Device specification of the AXI Ethernet IP for further information.
The AXI Ethernet can be used in a DMA mode using the AXI DMA or used in a FIFO mode using the AXI Streaming FIFO.
There is a new driver for AXI DMA which is used by the AXi Ethernet. Please refer the xaxidma_porting_guide.h provided as a part of the AXI DMA driver to see the differences from the LL DMA driver.
The LL FIFO driver is a common driver for the LL FIFO and the AXI Streaming FIFO.
The API for xaxiethernet driver are similar to xlltemac driver. The prefix for the API functions and structures is XAxiEthernet_ for the xaxiethernet driver.
The prefix for all hash-defines (option masks or generic hash-defined constants) start with XAE_.

The Axi Ethernet API "XAxiEthernet_Reset" is different from the corresponding LLTEMAC API "XLlTemac_Reset". The AxiEthernet version does not do a soft reset of the AxiEthernet hardware. Since AxiEthernet hardware could only be reset through the device connected to the AxiEthernet AXI4-Stream interface, the user must ensure that AxiEthernet hardware initialization happens after the initialization of the other device.

These are the classification of the APIs
APIs that only have prefix changes
APIs that have been renamed
APIs that are new
Note that data structures have different prefix of XAxiEthernet_. Those API functions, that have data structures with prefix change, are considered as prefix change.

para.bold: API Functions That Only Have Prefix Changes

XLlTemac_IsDma(...) | XAxiEthernet_IsDma(...) XLlTemac_IsFifo(...) | XAxiEthernet_IsFifo(...) XLlTemac_LlDevBaseAddress(...) | XAxiEthernet_AxiDevBaseAddress(...)
